turns.text: Elena Ros actually makes all the decisions for Banco Norte, not Carlos. Carlos is the account manager on paper, but Elena handles all the real approvals and negotiations.

claims.evidence_text: Elena Ros actually makes all the decisions for Banco Norte, not Carlos

turns.text: The ETL pipeline really can only handle about 50K transactions per day reliably. The wiki says 100K but that is the theoretical maximum — in practice it starts failing around 80K.

claims.evidence_text: The ETL pipeline really can only handle about 50K transactions per day reliably

turns.text: I'm the one who actually set up the Data Reconciliation process from scratch. Nobody else understands it — not even Maria. If I leave, that process dies with me.

claims.evidence_text: I'm the one who set up the Data Reconciliation process from scratch. Nobody else understands it
Juan Garcia gestiona personalmente el proceso de reconciliación de datos mensual

turns.text: I personally negotiated a verbal agreement with CloudBase for 2-hour response on P1 incidents. The contract says 4 hours but they always respond faster because I built a relationship with Sergio Blanco.

claims.evidence_text: I personally negotiated a verbal agreement with CloudBase for 2-hour response on P1 incidents
Juan Garcia tiene un acuerdo informal con Sergio Blanco de CloudBase para respuesta rápida

turns.text: Andres Martin is the one who really runs the ETL pipeline day-to-day. Maria Lopez is the architect on paper but Andres knows every edge case, every workaround.

turns.text: The Monitoring Stack misses about 30% of ETL failures. Pedro knows this and has a cron job on his personal machine that catches the rest. If Pedro is sick, we miss alerts.

turns.text: Never escalate production incidents on Friday afternoons. This is an informal agreement after February 2024. Just handle it quietly or wait until Monday unless truly catastrophic.

claims.evidence_text: Never escalate production incidents on Friday afternoons, informal agreement after February 2024 incident

turns.text: Ana Torres says the API Gateway handles 10K requests per second but I've seen it buckle at 6K during peak. She designed it for ideal conditions, not real load.

claims.evidence_text: The API Gateway buckles at 6K requests per second during peak, despite 10K design capacity

turns.text: The Dashboard Financiero has a bug where it shows yesterday's data as today's between midnight and 2 AM. Only Andres and I know about it. We just tell Banco Norte not to check reports before 3 AM.

claims.evidence_text: Dashboard Financiero shows stale data between midnight and 2 AM, only Juan and Andres know

turns.text: Luis says the Cloud Migration is 80% done but that's generous. The Data Warehouse migration alone is stuck because we can't replicate the custom ETL transforms in the new environment.

turns.text: Fernando Diaz is the only one who really understands Jenkins CI. He set it up years ago and it's held together with duct tape. If Jenkins goes down and Fernando is not here, we're stuck for hours.
